business.name: Aldrich & Stone LLP
business.stack_summary: 12-attorney boutique. Litigation + small-cap M&A. Boston. Bedrock-only Claude.

pantry.explanation: All matter content, pleadings, depositions, exhibits, drafts. Each matter is its own folder. Path-scoped walling enforces ethics: no agent reads across matters.

connection_methods.name: iManage REST API
connection_methods.install: iManage Cloud API key from admin. IMANAGE_API_KEY in ~/.env. Wrap in a Claude skill that respects the walling rules.

claude_code_recommendation.rationale: On matter open, sync the matter folder from iManage to local data/matters/<matter>/. Path-scoped rule then walls the agent from cross-matter reads.
claude_code_recommendation.estimated_time_savings: 30 min per matter open (today: copy/paste from iManage manually)

pantry.explanation: Client correspondence + opposing counsel. Matter-tagged via Outlook category. The conversion hook turns .eml/.msg into matter-walled markdown.

connection_methods.name: Mark's outlook skill (~/.claude/skills/outlook/)
connection_methods.when_to_use: Already wrapped at ~/.claude/skills/outlook/. Pulls categorized threads.

connection_methods.name: Microsoft Graph API
connection_methods.when_to_use: When you need full message bodies + attachments.
connection_methods.install: Azure app registration -> token. Already set up in your outlook skill.

claude_code_recommendation.skill_to_write: extend outlook skill with matter-categorized pull
claude_code_recommendation.rationale: Add a /matter-emails <matter_id> command that pulls all categorized threads into data/matters/<matter>/correspondence/.
claude_code_recommendation.estimated_time_savings: Eliminates the manual 'forward each thread to a matter folder' chore

pantry.explanation: Time entries by timekeeper / matter. Carol exports XLSX every Friday. The billing assistant reads the silver platter, drafts narratives for late entries.

connection_methods.name: Bill4Time REST API
connection_methods.when_to_use: Direct programmatic access to time entries.
cons: Bill4Time API is dated; REST + XML hybrid
API key managed in firm admin
connection_methods.install: BILL4TIME_API_KEY in ~/.env. Wrap as a Claude skill.

claude_code_recommendation.rationale: Pulls weekly time entries into silver_platters/billing_summary. Same-day conflict checks bake into the silver platter. Carol stops chasing partners for narratives.

pantry.explanation: Tablet intake scanned to PDF. Define the matter (client, type, conflicts, lead partner, scope). Drop into data/raw_dropzone/; conversion hook reads them.

connection_methods.name: Local file + conversion hook
connection_methods.when_to_use: Drop the intake PDF into data/raw_dropzone/.
cons: Sally has to remember to drop the file

claude_code_recommendation.rationale: On intake drop, the case-launch orchestrator scaffolds data/matters/<slug>/ with the standard skeleton (pleadings/, depositions/, exhibits/, correspondence/, BRIEF.md).

pantry.explanation: Research-only pilot. Doesn't feed matter operations. Mentioned for completeness; not part of the silver-platter chain today.

connection_methods.name: LexisNexis AI API
connection_methods.when_to_use: When you need primary-source case-law research the agent can cite.
cons: Not all attorneys have AI license
connection_methods.example: Used inside deposition-prep skill when a citation needs source-checking.

claude_code_recommendation.rationale: Wraps Lexis as a tool the deposition-prep specialist can call when citing case law.
claude_code_recommendation.estimated_time_savings: Saves the partner from manually verifying every cite

pantry.explanation: The IT-blessed deployment. Once Bedrock is signed, EVERY skill / agent / hook can run on it. This is the unlock.

connection_methods.name: AWS Bedrock invokeModel API
connection_methods.when_to_use: Required, no other Claude deployment is approved by IT for matter content.
cons: Higher per-token cost than direct Anthropic API
connection_methods.install: AWS account + Bedrock model access (request via AWS console). AWS_PROFILE in ~/.env. Configure Claude Code to use Bedrock backend.

claude_code_recommendation.skill_to_write: Configure Claude Code to use Bedrock (no skill, config change)
claude_code_recommendation.rationale: This is THE unlock. Without it, IT pulls the plug. Set it up first.

prep.name: matter_briefs/<matter>_brief.md
prep.explanation: One brief per active matter. Status, lead, opposing counsel, key issues, settlement posture, walling notes. The case-launch orchestrator's primary read.
prep.sample_content:
# Matter Brief, Smith v Jones (2024-CV-0883)

**Status:** OPEN, active litigation, summary judgment pending
**Lead attorney:** Marcus Stone (MS01)
**Second chair:** Sally T (AK01)
**Opposing counsel:** Daniel Chen, Chen Law Partners

## Posture
- Discovery substantially complete
- Jones deposed 2025-10-14 (transcript in data/matters/smith_v_jones/depositions/)
- MSJ filed 2025-09 on Counts II + III
- Opposition due 2025-11-21 (Sally drafting parts 1-2)

## Walling notes
WALLED from Acme acquisition (Marcus is lead on both, MS01 must check same-day conflicts).
No agent working Smith v Jones may read Acme files.
prep.governing_rule_excerpt:
---
paths:
  - data/matters/**
  - silver_platters/matter_briefs/**
---

# Matter Handling, THE HARD RULE

Each data/matters/<name>/ is a walled namespace. An agent working ONE matter NEVER reads files from another. If a question requires cross-matter knowledge, route through post-mortem distiller (closed matters only).
prep.subtitle_friendly: A Matters brief Claude writes, On matter open + weekly refresh

prep.name: billing_summary_<week>.md
prep.explanation: WIP by timekeeper, by matter. Same-day conflict checks. Drives Carol's billing-narrative drafter.
prep.sample_content:
# Billing Summary, W44

## WIP by timekeeper
| Timekeeper | Open hours W44 | Days late |
|---|---|---|
| Marcus Stone | 18.4 | 8.2 |
| Sally T | 16.5 | 3.6 |

## Same-day conflict alerts
- 2025-10-14: MS01 billed Smith v Jones (5.5h). Zero hours other matters. Clean.
prep.governing_rule_excerpt:
---
paths:
  - silver_platters/billing_*.md
  - data/billing/**
---

# Billing Format Rules
- Same-day conflict bar: no timekeeper bills across walled matters in the same calendar day without managing-partner approval.
prep.subtitle_friendly: A Billing brief Claude writes, Mon 7am

prep.name: post_mortem_lessons.md
prep.explanation: Cross-matter lessons. The Post-Mortem Distiller writes only on CLOSED matters, with redaction. The firm's institutional memory.
prep.sample_content:
# Lessons Learned, [Matter Name Redacted] (closed 2025-08)

1. Section 6.2 carve-out doctrine, defense's 'ordinary-course' framing was defeated by deposition admissions, not motion practice. Frontload deposition discovery on carve-out language.
2. Settlement posture, opposing counsel signaled their range BEFORE MSJ. We could have closed at 80% if we'd accepted before the motion landed.
3. Vendor [Redacted], billing escalations were inconsistent. For future engagements, lock the rate card and milestone schedule in the engagement letter.
prep.governing_rule_excerpt:
# Post-Mortem Distillation Rules

- Matter MUST be closed.
- All client-identifying details redacted: client name -> [Client], opposing party -> [Counterparty], specific dollar amounts -> ranges.
- Managing partner approves before firm-knowledge entry.
prep.subtitle_friendly: A Knowledge / lessons brief Claude writes, On matter close

plate.name: Deposition / focus brief
plate.explanation: Focus-areas brief from pleadings + depositions for the next motion or hearing. Marcus reads at 7am with coffee.
plate.sample_output:
# MSJ Opposition Focus, Smith v Jones

## Headline
The MSJ stands or falls on Section 6.2. The Jones deposition gave us three direct admissions that defeat the carve-out theory.

## Three admissions to anchor
1. Customer list disclosure (re: industry context vs list), supports tortious interference Count II.
2. Section 6.2 awareness ('I was aware of the Section, yes'), eliminates good-faith mistake defense.
3. Notice timing ('After' the demand letter), establishes pattern of post-hoc concealment.
ideation_loop: Marcus opens new matter -> case-launch orchestrator scaffolds data/matters/smith_v_jones/.
Marcus runs /prep_for_deposition smith_v_jones.
Case-Launch Orchestrator confirms matter scope (walled).
Routes to Deposition Prep Specialist with read-only access to that matter folder ONLY.
Deposition Prep reads the matter brief silver platter, plus all pleadings + depositions in the walled folder.
Drafts focus-areas brief with cited admissions + carve-out argument + count-by-count framing.
Returns to orchestrator. Orchestrator saves to outputs/deposition_prep/<matter>_<date>.md.
Marcus reviews + acknowledges in audit_log before circulation.
plate.subtitle_friendly: Written by the deposition-prep specialist for Marcus Stone, Sally

plate.name: Time-entry narrative drafts
plate.approval_gate: Timekeeper approves before submission to Bill4Time
plate.explanation: Drafts narratives for late entries from matter activity that day. Carol stops chasing.
plate.sample_output:
# Time-entry Drafts, W44, Marcus Stone

| Date | Matter | Hours | Drafted Narrative |
|---|---|---|---|
| 10/14 | Smith v Jones | 5.5 | Conducted deposition of Patricia Jones; primary examiner; covered carve-out admissions and notice-timing line. |
| 10/15 | Acme acq | 3.0 | Reviewed Acme due diligence package v3 with focus on IP assignments and open-source flagged packages; redlined definitive agreement. |
ideation_loop: Carol runs /weekly_billing on Friday 5pm.
Case-Launch Orchestrator routes to Billing Assistant.
Billing Assistant reads silver_platters/billing_summary_W44.md.
For each undrafted entry, reads the corresponding matter folder for that day's activity.
Drafts a tier-appropriate narrative (12-25 words; action verb + work product + context).
Same-day conflict check: confirms no timekeeper billed across walled matters same day.
Saves drafts to outputs/billing_drafts/W44_<timekeeper>.md.
Each timekeeper reviews + approves their own narratives. Carol pushes to Bill4Time.
plate.approval_friendly: Timekeeper approves before sending to billing
plate.subtitle_friendly: Written by the billing assistant for timekeepers

plate.name: Lessons learned (firm knowledge)
plate.approval_gate: Managing partner approves before firm-knowledge entry
plate.explanation: Closed matters only. Redacted. Builds the firm's institutional memory.
plate.sample_output:
# Lessons, [Counterparty] vs [Client] (closed 2025-08)

**Durable lessons:**
1. Carve-out language: deposition admissions defeat ordinary-course defense. Frontload the deposition.
2. Settlement before motion practice, accept signaled ranges if they're 70%+ of what trial would yield.
3. Vendor [Redacted], lock rate cards in engagement letter.

**Ephemeral content NOT included:**
- All correspondence
- All draft pleadings
- All billing entries
ideation_loop: Matter status flipped to closed in matter brief.
Janet runs /close_matter <matter>.
Case-Launch Orchestrator confirms matter is closed (refuses if open).
Routes to Post-Mortem Distiller (only allowed on closed matters).
Distiller reads the entire matter folder + brief.
Clusters content into ephemeral (drafts, correspondence) vs durable (strategic decisions, novel arguments).
Drafts lessons doc with full client redaction.
Saves to outputs/lessons_learned/<matter>_lessons.md.
Janet reviews. Approves. Lessons enter firm-knowledge.
plate.approval_friendly: Managing partner approves before firm-knowledge entry
plate.subtitle_friendly: Written by the post-mortem distiller for Janet Aldrich

recipes.name: New matter scaffolding (when intake comes in)
recipes.goal: Sally drops an intake PDF; ten minutes later the matter folder is scaffolded with brief, walling, and precedent links, instead of three hours of hand-rolling.

walkthrough.action: Drops the new client intake PDF into data/raw_dropzone/.
Converts the PDF into clean markdown in data/converted/intake_<client>.md.
Triggers the case-launch orchestrator.
Reads the converted intake. Generates matter slug. Creates data/matters/<slug>/ with the standard skeleton (pleadings/, depositions/, exhibits/, correspondence/).
Calls sync_imanage_matter to populate any precedent files from iManage.
Drafts silver_platters/matter_briefs/<slug>_brief.md with status, lead attorney, opposing counsel, conflicts, walling notes.
Adds the matter to the firm's master matter index.
Opens the matter brief, fills in any gaps, and starts working. Folder is walled by the path-scoped rule.

recipes.before_claude_code: Sally hand-rolls every new matter folder, hand-pulls precedent from iManage, hand-formats the case brief. 3 hours per matter. 4-5 matters a month.
recipes.after_claude_code: Drop the intake PDF, run /new_matter, the folder scaffolds in 10 minutes. Sally reviews and works.
recipes.headline: New matter scaffolding (when intake comes in)

recipes.name: Time-entry narratives (Friday 5pm)
recipes.goal: Carol stops chasing partners on Friday. Every late time entry has a drafted narrative ready for the timekeeper to approve.

walkthrough.action: Triggers ingest_bill4time_entries skill, pulls week's time entries via Bill4Time API.
Writes silver_platters/billing_summary_<week>.md with WIP by timekeeper + same-day conflict checks.
Triggers Case-Launch Orchestrator -> Billing Assistant.
For each undrafted entry, reads the matter folder for that day's activity (within the matter walling).
Drafts a 12-25 word narrative per entry: action verb + work product + context.
Flags any same-day conflict: refuses to draft narratives that cross walled matters in the same day without managing-partner approval.
Saves drafts to outputs/billing_drafts/<week>_<timekeeper>.md.

walkthrough.actor: Each timekeeper, Friday evening or Monday morning
walkthrough.action: Reviews own drafts. Approves. Carol pushes to Bill4Time.

recipes.before_claude_code: Time entries 6-8 days late on average. Carol chases partners with escalation pings. Money lost.
recipes.after_claude_code: Drafts ready Friday 5pm. Timekeepers approve in minutes. Days-late drops to 1-2.
recipes.headline: Time-entry narratives (Friday 5pm)

recipes.name: MSJ opposition focus brief (when motion lands)
recipes.goal: Marcus opens his iPad in the morning and sees the focus-areas brief for tomorrow's MSJ argument, three deposition admissions, the carve-out doctrine, the count-by-count framing.

walkthrough.action: Triggers Case-Launch Orchestrator with explicit matter scope.
Verifies matter scope (walled). Refuses if any cross-matter contamination. Routes to Deposition Prep Specialist.
Reads silver_platters/matter_briefs/smith_v_jones_brief.md plus every file in data/matters/smith_v_jones/pleadings/ and depositions/.
Identifies 3-5 deposition admissions that bear on the motion. Maps each to the relevant count.
Drafts the focus brief: headline + admissions + carve-out argument + alternative-pleading framing for each count + open questions for Marcus.
Saves to outputs/deposition_prep/<matter>_<date>.md with full citations.
Reads on iPad. Either circulates as-is or sends a one-line correction. Acknowledges in audit_log.

recipes.before_claude_code: Junior associates reinvent deposition prep for every motion. Three days of grunt work per brief.
recipes.after_claude_code: Brief lands overnight. Marcus reviews in 15 minutes. Three days reduced to one Marcus-review session.
recipes.headline: MSJ opposition focus brief (when motion lands)

recipes.name: Post-mortem distillation (matter close)
recipes.goal: When a matter closes, lessons get distilled into firm knowledge, separated from ephemeral case content, redacted, partner-approved. Knowledge stops dying at the bar.

walkthrough.actor: Janet, flips matter status to 'closed' in matter brief
walkthrough.action: Marker that the matter is eligible for distillation.
Triggers Case-Launch Orchestrator.
Verifies matter is closed (refuses if open). Routes to Post-Mortem Distiller.
Reads entire matter folder + brief. Clusters content into ephemeral (drafts, correspondence, billing) vs durable (strategic decisions, novel arguments, vendor experiences).
Applies redaction rules: client name -> [Client], opposing party -> [Counterparty], specific dollar amounts -> ranges, specific dates -> relative.
Drafts ~400-700 word lessons doc. Saves to outputs/lessons_learned/<matter>_lessons.md.
Warns Janet at session end if the lessons doc isn't acknowledged.
Adds [human_action] line to audit_log. Lessons doc enters firm-knowledge index.

recipes.before_claude_code: Post-mortems happened verbally at the bar after the matter closed. Knowledge died with the partner who held it.
recipes.after_claude_code: Every closed matter produces a redacted, approved lessons doc. The firm's institutional memory compounds.
recipes.headline: Post-mortem distillation (matter close)

setup_priority.title: Get IT sign-off, Bedrock 3P Claude deployment
setup_priority.what_to_do: Configure Claude Code to invoke models via AWS Bedrock instead of Anthropic.com. Sign Anthropic BAA. Get IT/security committee approval.
setup_priority.why: This is THE unlock. Without Bedrock, IT pulls the plug on every other step. No matter content can leave the firm tenant.
setup_priority.install:
# AWS account + Bedrock model access (request via AWS console)
# Set environment:
export ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL=https://bedrock-runtime.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
export AWS_PROFILE=aldrich_stone
# Configure Claude Code to use Bedrock backend
setup_priority.before: IT blocks every AI tool. The 15-user pilot is on borrowed time.
setup_priority.after: Claude Code runs on Bedrock. IT/security approves. Pilot expands to firm-wide.
setup_priority.setup_time: 1-2 weeks (mostly committee review). Community has the deeper walkthrough.
setup_priority.title_friendly: Get IT sign-off, Bedrock 3P Claude deployment

setup_priority.title: Path-scoped matter walling rule
setup_priority.what_to_do: Write rules/matter_handling.md with paths: data/matters/**. Defines that an agent reading one matter cannot access another in the same session.
setup_priority.why: Ethics walling is non-negotiable for litigation + M&A. Without this rule, the firm cannot use AI on client content.
setup_priority.install:
# write under .claude/rules/matter_handling.md
# YAML frontmatter:
# ---
# paths:
#   - data/matters/**
#   - silver_platters/matter_briefs/**
# ---
# Then the rule body (template in demos/02_boutique_law_firm/after/.claude/rules/)
setup_priority.before: Walling enforced by 'don't mix matters' instinct. Malpractice exposure.
setup_priority.after: Walling enforced by file system. Smith v Jones agent literally cannot load Acme files.

setup_priority.title: Wire the conversion hook
setup_priority.what_to_do: SessionStart hook that converts pleading PDFs, deposition DOCX, intake PDFs.eml correspondence into markdown.
setup_priority.why: Every matter document arrives as PDF or DOCX. Claude can't read these natively. This hook unblocks Step 4.
setup_priority.install:
cp -r skill/silver-platter/scripts/templates/.. ~/.claude/hooks/
brew install pandoc poppler
pip install xlsx2csv
setup_priority.before: Pleadings, depositions, intake forms, Claude fails on every one.
setup_priority.after: Drop any matter document into data/raw_dropzone/, get clean markdown in data/converted/.

setup_priority.title: Case-Launch Orchestrator + Billing Assistant + Deposition Prep + Post-Mortem Distiller
setup_priority.what_to_do: Define four subagents under .claude/agents/. Case-Launch is the chief-of-staff. The other three are matter-scoped specialists.
setup_priority.why: The orchestrator hierarchy is what makes the system usable. Sally and Marcus only talk to the orchestrator; specialists report up.
setup_priority.install:
# write under .claude/agents/:
#   case-launch-orchestrator.md (model: opus, tools: [Read, Glob, Grep, Task])
#   billing-assistant.md (model: sonnet)
#   deposition-prep-specialist.md (model: opus, tools include reading whole matter folders)
#   post-mortem-distiller.md (model: opus, redaction-aware)
# Hand to @claude-code-guide for the templates.
setup_priority.before: Sally has to pre-frame which folder Claude should look at, every time.
setup_priority.after: Sally runs /new_matter or /prep_for_deposition or /close_matter. The orchestrator routes.
setup_priority.title_friendly: Case-Launch Orchestrator + Billing Assistant + Deposition Prep + Post-Mortem Distiller

setup_priority.title: Audit log + approval-gate hooks
setup_priority.what_to_do: PostToolUse hook on Edit|Write that appends every action to outputs/audit_log.md. Stop hook that warns the partner at session end if a draft is unacknowledged.
setup_priority.why: For a regulated practice, the audit log is the difference between Bedrock approval and IT pulling the plug. Receipts.
setup_priority.install:
# Already in business_os_hooks.md reference doc.
# Paste the three hook entries into .claude/settings.json.
# chmod +x the three scripts in .claude/hooks/.
setup_priority.before: AI did what? Nobody knows. Did Marcus approve last week's brief? Carol can't tell.
setup_priority.after: Every action recorded. Stop hook nudges if a draft is unsigned. Bedrock review board sees clean audit trail.

setup_total_time: Bedrock approval is the long pole (1-2 weeks). Once Bedrock is live, the rest is a focused weekend.

opportunities.title: Bedrock + IT/security rollout walkthrough
opportunities.explanation: The Bedrock 3P Claude IT-unlock playbook (security memo, sample firm policy, IT-committee FAQs) lives in the Early AI Dopters community as a deeper dive.
opportunities.estimated_impact: Required for the firm to deploy at all

opportunities.title: Automate iManage matter sync
opportunities.explanation: Sync iManage matter folders to local data/matters/ on matter open. Skill calls iManage REST API.

opportunities.title: Build the Post-Mortem Distiller specialist
opportunities.explanation: Closed matters lose their lessons at the bar today. Distiller redacts client identifiers, drafts a 400-word lessons doc for managing-partner review.
opportunities.estimated_impact: Captures institutional knowledge that currently dies
